audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for overseers, servants/deacons, women/wives ambiguity, household conduct, mystery of godliness.

verses.restored: Trustworthy is the saying: if anyone reaches toward oversight, one desires a beautiful/good work.
Therefore the overseer must be above reproach, husband of one wife, sober, sound-minded, respectable, hospitable, skilled in teaching,
not given to wine, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, not lover of silver,
leading one's own household beautifully/well, having children in submission with all dignity,
but if someone does not know how to lead one's own household, how will one care for the assembly of the Divine?
not newly planted, lest being puffed up one fall into judgment of the adversarial one.
But it is also necessary to have a beautiful/good witness from those outside, so that one may not fall into reproach and snare of the adversarial one.
Servants/deacons likewise: dignified, not double-tongued, not given to much wine, not greedy for shameful gain,
holding the mystery of the trust in a pure conscience.
And let these also be tested first; then let them serve if they are blameless.
Women likewise, dignified, not slanderous, sober, trustworthy in all things.
Let servants/deacons be husbands of one wife, leading children and their own households beautifully/well.
For those who have served beautifully/well acquire a beautiful/good standing for themselves and much boldness in trust that is in Messiah Jesus.
These things I write to you, hoping to come to you soon,
but if I delay, so that you may know how it is necessary to conduct oneself in the household of the Divine, which is assembly of living Divine, pillar and support of the truth.

And confessedly great is the mystery of reverence/godliness: Who was manifested in flesh, made righteous/justified in Spirit, seen by messengers, proclaimed among nations, trusted in world, taken up in glory.

translation_notes: Episkope is oversight and episkopos overseer; the emphasis is character and care, not status hierarchy.
Diakonoi is servants/deacons. Verse 11 may refer to women deacons or wives; ambiguity is preserved as women likewise.
Mystery of godliness in 3:16 is likely hymnic/confessional; manifested in flesh is preserved without over-specifying textual variants in this first pass.
